rationale: A PowerShell process using a base64-encoded command (EV-001 parent) spawned cmd.exe to add a persistence entry under HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run pointing to C:\Path\[x].exe (A0, EV-002), establishing run-key persistence. Seconds later, a second encoded PowerShell command (EV-003 parent, decoded suffix '-Cleanup') spawned cmd.exe to silently delete the same registry value (EV-004, EV-005), a classic indicator of post-execution cleanup to evade detection. The full chain — encoded PowerShell → cmd → reg.exe add → encoded PowerShell cleanup → cmd → reg.exe delete, all running from the Temp directory at High integrity — is consistent with a staged malware or C2 implant performing persistence installation followed by artifact removal.
